response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates exceptional software engineering craftsmanship and breadth across full-stack development, they lack the depth required for this senior C++ systems role. Their last professional C++ work was 2015 (6 months at National Instruments), and they have no demonstrated expertise in core competencies: message brokers, MQTT, IoT systems, multi-threaded performance optimization, or industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443/SDL). The role requires a systems programmer ready to architect high-performance embedded systems; this candidate is an excellent web/full-stack generalist whose skills are misaligned with the domain.
strengths: Exceptional software engineering practices with strong emphasis on testing, TDD, and code quality demonstrated across 15+ years
Proven architectural thinking and ability to design large-scale distributed systems (GISCollective, microservices experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Demonstrated leadership and mentoring ability with community contributions, workshops, and technical talks to engineering audiences
concerns: C++ expertise is severely outdated and minimal (only 6 months professional experience in 2015; primary career in web/JavaScript/TypeScript/DLang)
Zero demonstrated expertise in required core domains: message brokers, MQTT protocol, IoT systems, multi-threaded performance optimization, and low-level networking
No background in industrial cybersecurity, Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL), IEC 62443 standards, or industrial automation systems—all central to the role

key_factors.factor: Message broker and IoT experience (MQTT, event-driven systems, protocol knowledge)
